title: unable to close conn
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Cannot close connection to container once run interactively

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run alpine container

        podman  run -it --rm docker.io/library/alpine /bin/sh

2. Then exit

        / # exit

This doesn't work and I have to hit Ctrl + C to exit.

**Describe the results you received:**

> ERRO[0002] unable to close conn: "close unixpacket @->/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/b96520808e7f77e03d4869f225c9841b9418863d9ea5b68f60a0fd008b41c40d/attach: use of closed network connection"

**Describe the results you expected:**

Exit container without having to run Ctrl + C

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
✗ podman version                                        
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

```

    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc

  SwapFree: 17119047680
```

```
✗ apt list podman

podman/xenial,now 1.6.2-1~ubuntu16.04~ppa1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
This works fine on Fedora 31, I would figure this is a problem with the conmon package and podman package interaction.

@giuseppe @haircommander @mheon Thoughts?
it seems like the latest conmon is installed but the version output seems incorrect (missing commit).  How was conmon installed?  It might just be an issue in the way we build the package
I think this is a build issue, so I am going to close, Reopen if I am mistaken.
For posterity; I can reliably recreate this issue on 1.6.3, but not on 1.7.0. Seems like that fixed it. It's also not ubuntu/mint specific.
